title: erigeron beyrichii
intro: Erigeron beyrichii, also known as the alpine fleabane, is a perennial plant species in the daisy family (Asteraceae) that is native to the mountainous regions of the Western United States. The plant has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been attributed to its high content of flavonoids and terpenoids. Erigeron beyrichii has been used to treat various health conditions, including wounds, sore throats, and digestive issues, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and promote tissue repair. Herbal preparations of Erigeron beyrichii can be found in the form of infusions, tinctures, and salves, which are often used topically or internally to harness its medicinal benefits.
